cards.back: A type of unsupervised machine learning algorithm that groups similar data points into k clusters based on their features

cards.front: What is the purpose of k-means clustering?
cards.back: To identify patterns, group similar data points, and reduce the dimensionality of a dataset

cards.front: What is a dataset for k-means clustering?
cards.back: A collection of data points with features that can be used to group similar data points into clusters

cards.front: How many clusters can k-means clustering have?
cards.back: The number of clusters is a parameter that must be specified before running the algorithm (k)

cards.front: What is the initialization step in k-means clustering?
cards.back: A random selection of initial cluster centers that are used to start the clustering process
